titleStmt.title: A Transcription of John in L329
GA L329
The New Testament
Das Neue Testament
The Gospel according to John
john

respStmt.name: the International Greek New Testament Project

titleStmt.funder: The Arts and Humanities Research Council

sponsor.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing

publisher.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing
University of Birmingham

availability.p: Licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License (CC BY 4.0): available for re-use provided attribution is made to the original creators (the IGNTP and any named participants) and an indication is given of any changes made. See further .

projectDesc.p: This transcription was made by the International Greek New Testament Project in its work towards the Editio Critica Maior of John between 2010 and 2013.

editorialDecl.p: The initial transcription file was a plain text file for use with COLLATE, later converted to XML. The original transcription guidelines can be found at
The plain text file was converted to XML by Catherine Smith using the project's Python conversion scripts 1.0.
The XML specifications used were version 1.4 of the IGNTP Guidelines available at

revisionDesc.change: Nom sac overline at 19.18 added by Rachel Kevern.
Ghost pages removed by Catherine Smith.
Word break at 18.9 corrected by Rachel Kevern.

body.note: This base text is divided into three sections:1. The Synaxarion. Readings are identified by 'S' for 'Synaxarion', 'W' for 'Week' and 'D' for 'Day' 2. Resurrection Readings are identified by 'R' for 'Resurrection3. The Menologion. Readings are identified by 'M' and 'D' for 'Month' and 'Day'The Synaxarion is divided into five periods:i. Pascha to Pentecost: S1 W1 D1 – S1 W8 D1ii. Pentecost to the Elevation of the Cross: S2 W1 D2 – S2 W18 D2iii. Elevation of the Cross to Lent: S3 W1 D2 – S3 W20 D1iv. Lent to Holy Week: S4 D1 D2 – S4 W7 D1cv. Holy Week: S5 W1 D2a – S5 W1 D7bStatus:G-Α number: 0329Νame of book transcribed: JohnFirst folio transcribed: F 1rΒase text: TRnsΤranscribed by: ASΤranscription begun: 18/9/12 Τranscription finished: 18/1/13 Does this manuscript contain (delete and complete as appropriate):Initials and other enlarged letters - Yes Diples (> or >> in the margin next to certain text, indicating that text is a quotation from the Old Testament) - No Are there any special symbols in the margin next to any of the following verses: 5.4 No 7.53 - 8.11 No Other remarks: 81v: U1, 91v: U2, U3, U4, 97r: U5 97v: U6, U7 108r: U8 109r: U9109v: U10 mismatch between heading and text111v: U11
1. συναξαριον i: πασχα to πεντηκοστη - ευαγγελια κατα ιωαννην - S1 W1 D1 - S1 W8 D1 Easter to Pentecost

div.note: The beginning of this lection (1.35ff.) is lacunose.

ab.note: βα overwritten by a later hand.

div.note: John 20,19-31 Second Sunday of Ρascha: Τhomas Sunday

ab.note: Some letters in this page have been rewritten by a later hand.
This word is rewritten as θαλαττης .
Some letters in this page have been rewritten by a later hand.

div.note: John 6,35-39 (According to Scrivener, Gale transposes with W3 D6)
John 6,48-54 (According to Scrivener, Gale transposes with W3 D4)
John 5,1-15 Fourth Sunday of Ρascha: Sunday of the Ρaralytic
John 4,5-42 Fifth Sunday of Ρascha: Sunday of the Samaritan Woman

ab.note: The ο and ς have been rewritten by a later hand.
Some letters on this folio have been rewritten by a later hand.
parts of the text on this folio are overwritten

fw.note: It is not clear whether this is μεγαλη or αγια.

div.note: κεφαλαιον [num]ρηζ[\num] is in the left margin.

ab.note: The scribe perhaps wrote ο before replacing it with η.

div.note: John 19,38-42, John 21,14-25 (Scrivener and Ρinakes omit John 21,14-25)
κεφαλαιον [num]ιϛ[\num] is in the left margin.

ab.note: 2. ευαγγελια εωθινα αναστασιμα R1 - R11 (Eleven Gospel Resurrection readings, one each Sunday at Matins beginning with All Saints' Day)

div.note: The heading instructs the user to read this pericope on the Sunday before the 14th September, the Feast of the Elevation of the Cross.
This lection is read on the Sunday that falls between 13th and 19th September in the Byzantine Church's calendar. It commemorates the Church councils.
